req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 5
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key axes for test design: (1) file format allow-list (PDF, PNG, JPG) vs. disallowed types, (2) file size boundary at 10 MB, and (3) 2FA confirmation as a prerequisite state. EP partitions valid formats vs. invalid formats and small vs. large files. BVA targets the exact 10 MB limit (9,999,999 B, 10,000,000 B, 10,000,001 B) — BVA is only used for boundary-kind tests. STC covers the 2FA gate state transitions — STC is only used for positive or boundary-kind tests that exercise state transitions. NEG covers oversized and wrong-MIME negative cases. DT combines format x size x 2FA columns for combined negative scenarios. Violations fixed: test[2] was negative+BVA — changed heuristic to NEG (off-point oversized file is a negative error pathway test, not a pure boundary test); test[4] was negative+STC — changed heuristic to NEG (the no-2FA block is a negative error-pathway test, not a state-transition coverage test). All other tests preserved unchanged.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_with_2fa
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
A valid PDF file of 1 MB is available
actions: POST /documents/upload with valid PDF (1 MB) and 2FA token header
Assert response body contains virus_scan_queued: true
trace: R-WEB-081: valid PDF under 10 MB accepted
R-WEB-081: virus scan queued after successful upload
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_with_2fa(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file,
    totp_token
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="pdf", size_bytes=1_000_000)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        headers={"X-2FA-Token": totp_token},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing from response"
    assert body.get("virus_scan_queued") is True, "virus scan not queued"

tests.name: test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_boundary_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
A PDF file of exactly 10,000,000 bytes is available
actions: POST /documents/upload with PDF of exactly 10,000,000 bytes and 2FA token
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 — file at exact limit is accepted
trace: R-WEB-081: upper boundary 10 MB inclusive must be accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_boundary_accepted(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file,
    totp_token
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="pdf", size_bytes=10_000_000)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        headers={"X-2FA-Token": totp_token},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected acceptance at boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body, "document_id missing at boundary upload"

tests.name: test_upload_over_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
A PDF file of 10,000,001 bytes is available
actions: POST /documents/upload with PDF of 10,000,001 bytes and 2FA token
expected: Response body indicates size limit exceeded
trace: R-WEB-081: error pathway 413 when size > 10 MB
R-WEB-081: NEG — file one byte over MAX_SIZE must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_over_10mb_returns_413(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file,
    totp_token
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="pdf", size_bytes=10_000_001)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        headers={"X-2FA-Token": totp_token},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413, (
        f"Expected 413 for oversized file, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
A TXT file of 100 KB is available
actions: POST /documents/upload with a .txt file (text/plain MIME) and 2FA token
expected: Response body indicates MIME type not in allow-list
trace: R-WEB-081: error pathway 415 when mime not in allow-list
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file,
    totp_token
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="txt", size_bytes=102_400)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("notes.txt", file_bytes, "text/plain")},
        headers={"X-2FA-Token": totp_token},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415, (
        f"Expected 415 for disallowed MIME, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_upload_blocked_without_2fa_confirmation
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
2FA confirmation has NOT been completed (token absent or invalid)
actions: POST /documents/upload with valid PDF (1 MB) and no X-2FA-Token header
trace: R-WEB-081: NEG — missing 2FA token must block upload with 401 or 403
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_blocked_without_2fa_confirmation(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="pdf", size_bytes=1_000_000)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")},
        # Intentionally omit X-2FA-Token header
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Expected 401/403 without 2FA, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" not in body, "document_id must not be returned without 2FA"

tests.name: test_upload_oversized_and_wrong_mime_returns_error
preconditions: User is authenticated via session cookie
A file of 12 MB with MIME text/csv is available
actions: POST /documents/upload with a CSV file (text/csv) of 12 MB and 2FA token
Assert HTTP 413 or 415 response (either size or MIME rejection fires first)
expected: HTTP 413 or 415 — at least one constraint violation triggers rejection
trace: R-WEB-081: DT row — invalid MIME AND oversized simultaneously
R-WEB-081: error pathways 413 and 415 both applicable
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_upload_oversized_and_wrong_mime_returns_error(
    authenticated_session,
    make_file,
    totp_token
):
    file_bytes = make_file(ext="csv", size_bytes=12_000_000)
    resp = authenticated_session.post(
        "/documents/upload",
        files={"file": ("data.csv", file_bytes, "text/csv")},
        headers={"X-2FA-Token": totp_token},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (413, 415), (
        f"Expected 413 or 415 for oversized+wrong MIME, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" not in body, "document_id must not be returned on combined violation"

EP partitions valid formats vs. invalid formats and small vs. large files. BVA targets the exact 10 MB limit (9,999,999 B, 10,000,000 B, 10,000,001 B). STC covers the 2FA gate: unauthenticated-2FA → authenticated-2FA → upload allowed. DT combines format × size × 2FA columns. NEG covers oversized and wrong-MIME cases.
